symptoms: works if the connector is wiggled

aoi_blockers: scan quality is below production threshold (0.50, tracked)
image-only topology confidence is below production threshold
calibrated camera/lighting fixture and focus checks
labeled defect dataset from the target production line
model/license review for any sourced checkpoints before redistribution

rules: start every powered test with a current-limited supply when possible
label connector polarity and voltage before reconnecting loads
treat unexpected heating as a fault; power only briefly under current limit while measuring
test inductive loads with flyback/protection in place

scan_matches.function: Accepts incoming power and protects downstream circuitry
Converts, filters, or stabilizes board power rails
Switches higher current loads from low-power control signals
Accepts incoming power and protects downstream circuitry
Provides external electrical connections to a machine or host
Converts, filters, or stabilizes board power rails

fault_candidates.name: Driver stage or motor/load path fault
likely_causes: open motor winding or disconnected harness
missing flyback/protection diode causing repeated driver damage

diagnostic_tests.name: Load resistance and isolation check
steps: Disconnect power and unplug the motor/load harness.
Measure resistance across the motor/load leads.
Measure each motor/load lead to board ground.
diagnostic_tests.expected_result: Finite winding/load resistance and no short to ground unless designed that way.
diagnostic_tests.fault_indicated_if: Near-zero resistance, open circuit, or unexpected ground short.

diagnostic_tests.name: Driver output test with current limit
steps: Power the board through a current-limited supply.
Command the output while using a dummy load before reconnecting the real motor.
Measure output voltage across the load terminals.
diagnostic_tests.expected_result: Output switches cleanly without current-limit collapse.
diagnostic_tests.fault_indicated_if: No switching, supply current limit trips, or driver overheats.

repair_steps: Reseat or replace damaged harness/connectors.
Replace failed MOSFET/transistor/relay with equal or higher voltage/current rating.
Replace or add flyback/protection diode for inductive loads.
Retest with dummy load before reconnecting the motor or external load.

fault_candidates.name: Power input, protection, or regulator fault
likely_causes: bad adapter, cable, fuse, switch, or jack
failed regulator or dried output capacitor

diagnostic_tests.name: Input-to-rail power tree check
steps: Identify input voltage and polarity before powering.
Measure resistance from main rail to ground with power disconnected.
Power through a current limit and measure input, regulator input, and regulator output.
diagnostic_tests.expected_result: Input and regulated rails match expected voltages within tolerance.
diagnostic_tests.fault_indicated_if: Shorted rail, zero regulator output, excessive current draw, or unstable rail.

repair_steps: Replace damaged cable/jack/fuse/switch first.
If regulator input is present but output is wrong, replace regulator and nearby capacitors.
If rail is shorted, isolate loads until the short disappears.
Retest under current limit before normal power.

fault_candidates.name: Connector, solder joint, or harness intermittency

diagnostic_tests.name: Wiggle and continuity test
steps: Power off and inspect connector solder joints under magnification.
Measure continuity from connector pin to next visible node.
Flex the cable gently while observing continuity.
diagnostic_tests.expected_result: Stable continuity with no visual cracking.
diagnostic_tests.fault_indicated_if: Continuity drops, joint ring cracks are visible, or cable movement changes behavior.

repair_steps: Clean contacts with electronics-safe contact cleaner.
Reflow cracked connector joints with flux.
Replace damaged cable or connector shell.
Add strain relief before final reassembly.

diagnostic_flow.title: Document before touching
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Preserve connector orientation, labels, screws, and cable routing.
actions: photograph all sides, connectors, switch positions, and labels
record symptoms exactly and whether they are intermittent
mark unknown connectors before unplugging them
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: device state and wiring are reproducible

diagnostic_flow.title: Unpowered inspection
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Find obvious mechanical, thermal, liquid, or solder damage without adding risk.
actions: inspect connectors, cables, switches, and battery compartment
look for burned areas, cracked solder joints, corrosion, bulged capacitors, and missing parts
compare to scan/AOI defect candidates when available
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: repair visible damage or quarantine unsafe battery/high-voltage assembly first

diagnostic_flow.title: Power path check
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm that input power reaches the board and regulated rails are not shorted.
actions: measure input connector polarity and voltage
measure rail-to-ground resistance before applying power
power with current limit and check regulator outputs
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: rails are present, stable, and not current-limited

diagnostic_flow.title: Output/load isolation
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Separate a bad board driver from a bad motor, relay, solenoid, cable, or load.
actions: disconnect the load harness
measure load resistance and check for shorts to chassis/ground
test the board output with a dummy load before reconnecting the real load
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: dummy load switches correctly and real load measures sane resistance

diagnostic_flow.title: Confirm candidate: Driver stage or motor/load path fault
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm the suspected fault before replacing parts.
actions: Disconnect power and unplug the motor/load harness.
Measure resistance across the motor/load leads.
Measure each motor/load lead to board ground.
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: Finite winding/load resistance and no short to ground unless designed that way.
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: Near-zero resistance, open circuit, or unexpected ground short.

diagnostic_flow.title: Confirm candidate: Power input, protection, or regulator fault
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm the suspected fault before replacing parts.
actions: Identify input voltage and polarity before powering.
Measure resistance from main rail to ground with power disconnected.
Power through a current limit and measure input, regulator input, and regulator output.
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: Input and regulated rails match expected voltages within tolerance.
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: Shorted rail, zero regulator output, excessive current draw, or unstable rail.

diagnostic_flow.title: Confirm candidate: Connector, solder joint, or harness intermittency
diagnostic_flow.purpose: Confirm the suspected fault before replacing parts.
actions: Power off and inspect connector solder joints under magnification.
Measure continuity from connector pin to next visible node.
Flex the cable gently while observing continuity.
diagnostic_flow.pass_condition: Stable continuity with no visual cracking.
diagnostic_flow.fail_branch: Continuity drops, joint ring cracks are visible, or cable movement changes behavior.

repair_playbooks.title: Driver stage or motor/load path fault
repair_steps: Reseat or replace damaged harness/connectors.
Replace failed MOSFET/transistor/relay with equal or higher voltage/current rating.
Replace or add flyback/protection diode for inductive loads.
Retest with dummy load before reconnecting the motor or external load.
confirmation_after_repair: run a short thermal check under normal load
record the repair and replaced parts

repair_playbooks.title: Power input, protection, or regulator fault
repair_steps: Replace damaged cable/jack/fuse/switch first.
If regulator input is present but output is wrong, replace regulator and nearby capacitors.
If rail is shorted, isolate loads until the short disappears.
Retest under current limit before normal power.
confirmation_after_repair: run a short thermal check under normal load
record the repair and replaced parts

repair_playbooks.title: Connector, solder joint, or harness intermittency
repair_steps: Clean contacts with electronics-safe contact cleaner.
Reflow cracked connector joints with flux.
Replace damaged cable or connector shell.
Add strain relief before final reassembly.
confirmation_after_repair: run a short thermal check under normal load
record the repair and replaced parts

when_to_stop: battery is swollen, punctured, hissing, smoking, or unusually hot
you cannot identify the input voltage or polarity
the board immediately hits current limit after disconnecting all loads
a replacement part rating cannot be verified
the repair requires live mains probing and you do not have the proper equipment
evidence_to_collect_next: current draw at startup under current limit
limitations: This guide is a diagnostic workflow, not proof of a fault until measurements confirm it.
Do not probe mains-powered equipment live. Isolate high voltage before board-level inspection.
Image-only PCB inference cannot see hidden layers, bottom-side components, or exact manufacturer revisions.
quick_summary: Small DC motor / actuator gadget: start with Document before touching; top candidate is Driver stage or motor/load path fault.
